recommendation: When revenue drop concentrated in one region or segment: investigate regional_order_volume, regional_aov, regional_churn in that order to identify the root cause.

trigger_condition: CLV up in one channel only
recommendation: When CLV up in one channel only: investigate that acquisition channel is attracting higher-quality customers — verify with ec_new_vs_returning segmented by utm_source or channel in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When repeat_rate up, ec_clv flat: investigate customers are buying more often but spending less per order — check ec_aov trend to confirm whether basket size is shrinking in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what percentage of customers come back

trigger_condition: repeat_rate down, ec_new_vs_returning shows new customer share increasing
recommendation: When repeat_rate down, ec_new_vs_returning shows new customer share increasing: investigate acquisition is outpacing retention — the denominator is growing with new customers who have not yet had time to repeat in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what percentage of customers come back

recommendation: When repeat_rate up, ec_cohort_retention flat: investigate the improvement may be a time-window artifact — more customers crossed the repeat threshold in this measurement period, but the underlying retention curve has not changed in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what percentage of customers come back

recommendation: When repeat_rate down across all channels: investigate systemic issue — check for product quality problems, shipping delays (logi_on_time_delivery_rate), or competitive pressure rather than a channel-specific cause in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what percentage of customers come back

trigger_condition: new customer share rising, ec_gmv also rising
recommendation: When new customer share rising, ec_gmv also rising: investigate growth is acquisition-driven — verify marketing spend increased and check if these new customers have lower ec_clv than historical cohorts in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: new customer share rising, ec_gmv flat
recommendation: When new customer share rising, ec_gmv flat: investigate new customers are replacing churning returning customers at roughly the same rate — the business is on a treadmill, not growing in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: returning customer share rising, ec_repeat_rate also rising
recommendation: When returning customer share rising, ec_repeat_rate also rising: investigate genuine retention improvement — customers are buying more often, shifting the mix toward returning in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When new customer share drops suddenly: investigate acquisition channel disruption — check mkt_cac and marketing spend for the same period to identify which channel declined in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When high-R-low-F segment is growing: investigate new customers are being acquired but not converting to repeat buyers — check ec_repeat_rate and onboarding flows in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When low-R-high-F-high-M segment is growing: investigate previously loyal high-value customers are becoming dormant — a churn risk signal that should trigger retention outreach in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: the 555 segment shrinks while 111 segment grows
recommendation: When the 555 segment shrinks while 111 segment grows: investigate overall customer quality is declining — likely driven by acquisition channel mix shifts, verify with ec_new_vs_returning by channel in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: monetary score distribution compresses (all scores cluster around 3)
recommendation: When monetary score distribution compresses (all scores cluster around 3): investigate the customer base has become more homogeneous in spending — either pricing standardized or outlier B2B accounts were removed in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: cohort retention up, ec_clv also up
recommendation: When cohort retention up, ec_clv also up: investigate genuine improvement — customers are staying longer and generating more lifetime revenue in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When cohort retention flat, ec_repeat_rate up: investigate existing repeat customers are buying more frequently, but the rate of first-to-second conversion has not improved — the improvement is in frequency, not breadth of retention in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how many customers come back each month

trigger_condition: cohort retention down in period 1 only
recommendation: When cohort retention down in period 1 only: investigate the first-purchase experience is failing to drive a second purchase — check product quality, shipping experience (logi_on_time_delivery_rate), or post-purchase email sequences in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: cohort retention declining for recent cohorts but stable for older ones
recommendation: When cohort retention declining for recent cohorts but stable for older ones: investigate acquisition quality has degraded — recent cohorts are lower-intent customers, possibly from new channels or heavy discounting in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When CAC up, conversion_rate down: investigate spend is inefficient — likely poor targeting, creative fatigue, or channel saturation; verify ad frequency and click-through trends in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When CAC up, new_customer_count flat: investigate spend increased without more conversions — likely lower-quality leads or less effective channels in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When CAC down, new_customer_count down: investigate spend reduced *and* conversions dropped disproportionately — likely cutting channels that drive incremental volume in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When TBO down, repeat_buy_rate up: investigate more frequent repurchases — likely successful replenishment campaigns, consumable product mix, or improved fulfillment timing in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When TBO up, return_rate up: investigate longer intervals reflect poor satisfaction — customers return items and delay reordering, possibly due to quality or fit issues in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When TBO up, churn_rate up: investigate customers are less engaged — likely product obsolescence, competitive displacement, or failing win-back programs in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Win-back rate up, churn_rate down: investigate win-back efforts are reducing net churn — likely effective re-engagement messaging or product re-alignment in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Win-back rate up, marketing_spend up: investigate incremental spend drove reactivation — likely successful new channel or creative testing in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When Win-back rate down, churn_rate up: investigate product or service issues causing more customers to leave and fewer to return — likely declining satisfaction or competitive threat in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When F2S conversion up, first_order_count up: investigate more first-time buyers + more repeat buyers — likely successful acquisition campaigns or improved product appeal in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When F2S conversion up, churn_rate down: investigate early adopters are becoming retained — likely effective onboarding, product satisfaction, or win-back timing in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When F2S conversion down, first_order_count down: investigate fewer first-time buyers, and fewer converting to second — likely declining brand visibility, competitive pressure, or product quality in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Concentration risk up, revenue growth up
recommendation: When Concentration risk up, revenue growth up: investigate growth driven by large customers — likely strategic expansion but increased vulnerability to churn or negotiation in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Concentration risk up, churn_rate up: investigate top customers are volatile — likely over-reliance on key accounts without broadening base in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Concentration risk down, revenue growth down
recommendation: When Concentration risk down, revenue growth down: investigate diversification came at the cost of top-end growth — likely losing high-value customers or shrinking enterprise segment in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When conversion_rate up, ec_gmv also up: investigate more visitors are buying — likely a UX improvement, better product-market fit, or a successful promotion that increased purchase intent in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When conversion_rate up, ec_gmv flat: investigate conversion improved but traffic volume decreased proportionally — check if a paid channel was cut (fewer but higher-intent visitors remaining) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When conversion_rate down, ec_cart_abandonment up: investigate users are adding to cart but not completing checkout — the friction is in the checkout flow, not in product discovery or consideration in that order to identify the root cause.

trigger_condition: conversion_rate down, session count up dramatically
recommendation: When conversion_rate down, session count up dramatically: investigate traffic quality degraded — a new traffic source is sending high-volume, low-intent visitors that dilute CVR. Check traffic source mix. in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When cart_abandonment up, ec_conversion_rate down: investigate the same demand is being lost — users show purchase intent (add to cart) but fail to complete, directly reducing conversion in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When cart_abandonment flat, ec_conversion_rate down: investigate the conversion drop is upstream of add-to-cart — fewer sessions are reaching the cart stage. Check product discovery, search, and browsing experience. in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: shipping step drop-off increases, ec_cart_abandonment also increases
recommendation: When shipping step drop-off increases, ec_cart_abandonment also increases: investigate users are seeing unexpected shipping costs or delivery estimates and abandoning — check if shipping rates or delivery time messaging changed in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When payment step drop-off spikes suddenly: investigate payment processing issue — gateway outage, new fraud rules, or expired payment processor credentials. Check payment error logs. in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: begin_checkout drop-off increases but cart_to_checkout conversion is stable
recommendation: When begin_checkout drop-off increases but cart_to_checkout conversion is stable: investigate users are adding to cart but choosing not to begin checkout — likely using cart as a wishlist or comparison tool, not a purchase signal in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: all step-to-step rates stable but overall funnel volume drops
recommendation: When all step-to-step rates stable but overall funnel volume drops: investigate upstream traffic volume declined — the funnel efficiency is unchanged but fewer users are entering it. Check ec_conversion_rate and session counts. in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When sessions_to_purchase increases, ec_conversion_rate decreases: investigate customers are taking longer to decide, which means more sessions exist in the denominator without converting — this is natural and does not necessarily indicate a problem if total conversions are stable in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When sessions_to_purchase decreases, ec_aov also decreases: investigate the business may be shifting toward lower-priced impulse products that require less consideration — verify product category mix in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: sessions_to_purchase increases for a specific category only
recommendation: When sessions_to_purchase increases for a specific category only: investigate that category may have introduced higher-priced items, more complex products, or reduced trust signals (fewer reviews, less product detail) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: sessions_to_purchase drops after a site change
recommendation: When sessions_to_purchase drops after a site change: investigate improved product information, better search, or streamlined UX reduced the need for multiple research visits — but verify this is not just a tracking change that merged sessions in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: PDP to cart rate up, conversion_rate up
recommendation: When PDP to cart rate up, conversion_rate up: investigate product page optimizations are driving more *and* deeper engagement — likely improved imagery, reviews, or pricing transparency in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: PDP to cart rate up, AOV down
recommendation: When PDP to cart rate up, AOV down: investigate more items added per order but lower value — likely upsell mechanics failing or promotional bundling shifting mix in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: PDP to cart rate down, revenue per visitor up
recommendation: When PDP to cart rate down, revenue per visitor up: investigate fewer cart adds but higher value per convert — likely more qualified traffic or better targeting, not page issues in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Shipping-to-Billing drop-off up, shipping_cost up: investigate unexpected or increased shipping cost is driving abandonment — likely a change in carrier rates or delivery policy in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Review-to-Payment drop-off up, new_payment_methods added: investigate new payment methods may lack trust or user familiarity — likely technical friction or incomplete implementation in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Cart-to-Shipping drop-off up, cart_value up: investigate high-value carts fear commitment — likely account creation friction or lack of guest checkout option in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Payment failure rate up, checkout_step_drop_off at payment up
recommendation: When Payment failure rate up, checkout_step_drop_off at payment up: investigate payment gateway issues (e.g., rate limits, maintenance) causing failures — likely technical, not customer-side in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Payment failure rate up, new_card_rate up
recommendation: When Payment failure rate up, new_card_rate up: investigate new card introductions (e.g., post-launch, promotions) with unfamiliar BINs are triggering fraud blocks in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Payment failure rate down, fraud_rate up
recommendation: When Payment failure rate down, fraud_rate up: investigate less aggressive fraud rules now allow more transactions — likely a balance shift toward lower rejection and higher true fraud in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Search-to-purchase rate up, search_volume up: investigate more search users + better quality — likely improved search UX or product catalog alignment in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Search-to-purchase rate down, search_volume up: investigate search traffic growth is lower quality — likely marketing campaigns driving untargeted traffic or keyword stuffing in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Search-to-purchase rate up, revenue per visitor down
recommendation: When Search-to-purchase rate up, revenue per visitor down: investigate search-driven purchases are lower value — likely discount-driven searches (e.g., 'cheap') or high-add-to-cart, low-checkout behavior in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Cross-sell attach rate up, AOV up
recommendation: When Cross-sell attach rate up, AOV up: investigate cross-selling is driving higher basket size — likely successful recommendation algorithm or bundling strategy in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Cross-sell attach rate down, AOV up
recommendation: When Cross-sell attach rate down, AOV up: investigate fewer categories but higher value per category — likely premiumization (e.g., high-price单品) or category focus in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Cross-sell attach rate up, return_rate up
recommendation: When Cross-sell attach rate up, return_rate up: investigate more diverse orders are harder to match expectations — likely over-selling or mismatched recommendations causing returns in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When inventory_turnover up, ec_stockout_rate also up: investigate inventory is turning faster because it is chronically understocked — the business is selling through but losing sales to stockouts on popular items in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how quickly do we sell inventory

recommendation: When inventory_turnover down, ec_revenue_by_category flat: investigate inventory levels grew without a corresponding sales increase — likely over-purchasing or slow-moving new SKUs added to the catalog in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how quickly do we sell inventory

recommendation: When inventory_turnover up, ec_gmv up: investigate genuine demand increase is pulling inventory through faster — healthy signal if not accompanied by rising stockouts in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how quickly do we sell inventory

trigger_condition: inventory_turnover down in one category only
recommendation: When inventory_turnover down in one category only: investigate that category has excess stock — check for seasonal products past their peak, discontinued items not yet written off, or a demand forecast miss in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how quickly do we sell inventory

recommendation: When DIO up, ec_inventory_turnover down: investigate these are mathematical inverses — they are describing the same phenomenon. Look for the underlying cause in purchasing, demand, or dead stock. in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When DIO up, ec_stockout_rate flat: investigate the business is building excess inventory without demand to match — over-purchasing rather than a demand problem in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When DIO down, ec_stockout_rate up: investigate inventory is depleting faster than it is replenished — the business is selling through but running out of stock on popular items in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: DIO up in one category, ec_revenue_by_category for that category declining
recommendation: When DIO up in one category, ec_revenue_by_category for that category declining: investigate demand is shifting away from that category but purchasing has not adjusted — reduce reorder quantities for that category in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: product_affinity between two items increases after a cross-sell promotion
recommendation: When product_affinity between two items increases after a cross-sell promotion: investigate the promotion is working — verify by checking if the lift also increased, not just raw co-purchase count (which could increase from overall volume growth) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: product_affinity drops for a historically strong pair
recommendation: When product_affinity drops for a historically strong pair: investigate one of the products may be out of stock (ec_stockout_rate), discontinued, or replaced by a competitor product in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: high-affinity pairs have very different ec_aov contributions
recommendation: When high-affinity pairs have very different ec_aov contributions: investigate the lower-value item is an accessory or complement — it may not drive margin but increases basket size and reduces shipping cost per item in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: category-level affinity increases but product-level is flat
recommendation: When category-level affinity increases but product-level is flat: investigate customers are buying from the same categories together but substituting between specific products — useful for category page cross-sell but not product page recommendations in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: one category's revenue share grows while absolute revenue is flat
recommendation: When one category's revenue share grows while absolute revenue is flat: investigate other categories are declining — the growing share is relative, not from actual growth in that category in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: ec_revenue_by_category shifts toward higher-AOV categories, ec_aov rises
recommendation: When ec_revenue_by_category shifts toward higher-AOV categories, ec_aov rises: investigate the AOV increase is a mix effect — customers are not buying more per order, they are buying from pricier categories in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: a category's revenue drops while ec_stockout_rate for that category rises
recommendation: When a category's revenue drops while ec_stockout_rate for that category rises: investigate the category is losing revenue to stockouts — demand exists but cannot be fulfilled due to inventory shortages in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: category revenue grows but ec_product_affinity pairs involving that category weaken
recommendation: When category revenue grows but ec_product_affinity pairs involving that category weaken: investigate the category may be cannibalizing adjacent categories rather than generating incremental cross-sell revenue in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When stockout_rate up, ec_gmv down: investigate lost sales from unavailable products — estimate the impact by multiplying stockout SKU revenue velocity by the duration of stockout in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: how often are products out of stock

recommendation: When stockout_rate up, ec_inventory_turnover up: investigate inventory is depleting faster than replenishment — either demand spiked or reorder process failed in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: stockout_rate up in one category, ec_revenue_by_category for that category declining

expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Overlap rate up, conversion_rate down: investigate internal competition reducing efficiency — likely audience saturation or fatigue from repeated exposure in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Overlap rate up, spend up: investigate more campaigns running simultaneously — likely expanded campaign portfolio without targeting alignment in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Overlap rate down, ROI down: investigate reduced overlap but lower performance — likely under-targeting or fragmented audience reach in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Incrementality up, conversion_rate down: investigate campaign is driving incremental conversions — likely new customers or new behavior, not cannibalization in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Incrementality up, spend up: investigate campaign is driving new volume — likely growth or market expansion in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Incrementality down, conversion_rate down: investigate campaign effectiveness declined — likely creative fatigue, targeting decay, or competitive pressure in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Fatigue point down, conversion_rate down: investigate ads are less effective at frequency — likely creative fatigue or audience saturation in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Fatigue point down, spend up: investigate more spending without efficiency — likely overexposure and diminishing returns in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Fatigue point up, conversion_rate up: investigate ads more effective at frequency — likely improved creative or targeting in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: Landing page CR up, ROAS up
recommendation: When Landing page CR up, ROAS up: investigate landing page improvements driving better ROI — likely optimized UX, copy, or offer alignment in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what is my landing page CR

trigger_condition: Landing page CR down, conversion_rate up
recommendation: When Landing page CR down, conversion_rate up: investigate landing page underperforms but overall funnel improves — likely traffic quality shift (e.g., better channel mix) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what is my landing page CR

trigger_condition: Landing page CR down, bounce_rate up
recommendation: When Landing page CR down, bounce_rate up: investigate landing page quality declined — likely UX issues, slow load time, or misaligned messaging in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: what is my landing page CR

recommendation: When DAU/MAU up, session_length down: investigate users are visiting more often but each visit is shorter — likely a feature launch driving quick-check behavior (e.g., mobile app push notifications) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: daily active users monthly active users

recommendation: When DAU/MAU down, churn_rate up: investigate users are dropping off more quickly between sessions — early warning of engagement erosion or product-market-fit issues in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: daily active users monthly active users

trigger_condition: DAU/MAU up only in new users, not returning
recommendation: When DAU/MAU up only in new users, not returning: investigate acquisition channel worked but retention is broken — onboarding or first-value experience needs review in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: daily active users monthly active users

recommendation: When DAU/MAU flat but MAU down: investigate core active users are retreating — possible churn signal despite stable frequency in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause
tags: daily active users monthly active users

recommendation: When session_length down, feature_usage_rate down: investigate core content is less engaging — check for content quality issues, layout changes, or algorithmic filtering (e.g., less recommended items shown) in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When session_length up, churn_rate down: investigate deeper engagement is sticky — likely caused by a successful onboarding or feature enhancement in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When session_length flat, DAU/MAU up: investigate more users are visiting, but engagement per visit unchanged — likely acquisition growth, not product improvement in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

trigger_condition: session_length up in one segment only
recommendation: When session_length up in one segment only: investigate a localized feature test (e.g., video autoplay for mobile) improved that cohort's depth without affecting others in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When feature_usage_rate up, session_length up: investigate users are engaging deeper with the feature — likely indicating real utility, not just surface-level clicks in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When feature_usage_rate up, churn_rate down: investigate the feature is sticky — early evidence of retention lift; monitor longitudinal impact in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When feature_usage_rate flat, DAU/MAU up: investigate new users are signing up but not adopting the feature — onboarding or discovery friction in first session in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When feature_usage_rate down, support_tickets up: investigate users are struggling with the feature — likely UI/UX issue or unclear value proposition in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Feature adoption up, retention up: investigate feature is stickiness driver — likely increasing engagement or solving a key user need in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Feature adoption up, feature_breadth down: investigate users focus on this feature — likely feature maturity or reduced exploratory usage in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Feature adoption down, feature_breadth up: investigate users explore more features — likely onboarding success or reduced feature fatigue in that order to identify the root cause.
expected_impact: Identify and isolate the root cause

recommendation: When Session depth up, retention up: investigate users engaging more deeply are staying longer — likely meaningful engagement driving retention in that order to identify the root cause.

recommendation: When Session depth down, bounce_rate up: investigate users less engaged — likely UI issues, slow load time, or irrelevant content in that order to identify the root cause.
